key_finding: tcrLM, a lightweight masked language model pretrained on TCR CDR3 sequences, accurately predicts TCR-peptide binding specificity and shows correlation between predicted TCR-neoantigen binding scores and immunotherapy response in melanoma patients.
title: A lightweight TcrLM model predicts T cell receptor and epitope binding specificity.

technology: high-throughput sequencing of TCR and BCR repertoires
key_finding: Tacrolimus therapy induces greater remodeling of the B-cell receptor repertoire than the T-cell receptor repertoire in myasthenia gravis patients, with potential downregulation of specific immunoglobulin V gene families.
title: Longitudinal omics reveals immune repertoire remodeling in myasthenia gravis patients post-tacrolimus therapy.

key_finding: Systemic immune profiling identifies divergent immune mechanisms and predictive biomarkers of response to combination immunotherapies (atezolizumab plus bevacizumab and durvalumab plus tremelimumab) in advanced hepatocellular carcinoma.
title: Systemic immune profiling uncovers divergent mechanisms and predictive biomarkers of response to combination immunotherapies in hepatocellular carcinoma.

key_finding: T cell clonal dynamics reveal two distinct immune suppression patterns in MSI and MSS colorectal cancers that better explain immunotherapy response than MSI status alone.
title: Single-Cell Multi-Tissue T Cell Clonal Dynamics Reveal Distinct Immune Coercion Landscapes in MSI and MSS Colorectal Cancer.

key_finding: Vitamin D promotes immune tolerance to gut microbiota in patients with IBD by increasing IgA-bound bacteria, enhancing BAFF signaling, and expanding regulatory T and B cells.
title: Multi-omics reveal vitamin D regulation of immune-gut microbiome interactions and tolerogenic pathways in inflammatory bowel disease.

key_finding: Vaccination and infection produce subtle, diffuse B- and T-cell repertoire signatures with distinct age effects, where TCR binding capacity to SARS-CoV-2-specific sequences effectively predicts neutralizing antibody levels.
title: Contrasting effects of SARS-CoV-2 vaccination vs. infection on antibody and TCR repertoires.

key_finding: A TCR signature in peripheral blood CD4+ T cells is associated with chronic chikungunya disease and suggests a female-biased, conducive background immune profile.
title: Identification of a TCR signature in peripheral blood derived CD4+ T cells, associated with chronic chikungunya disease, suggests a conducive, female-biased, background immune profile.

key_finding: Single-cell RNA sequencing reveals the transcriptional landscape and characterizes CD4+ T cell subpopulations in systemic sclerosis patients, providing insights into the cellular and molecular mechanisms of the disease.
title: Transcriptional landscape of CD4+ T cells in Systemic Sclerosis.

key_finding: Combining neoantigen vaccination with genomic profiling via NGS represents a promising personalized medicine approach for TNBC patients undergoing neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
title: Personalized medicine in triple-negative breast cancer: combining neoantigen vaccination and genomic profiling in a patient undergoing neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

key_finding: GATTCR, a Graph Attention Network framework with multi-feature fusion, achieves superior TCR repertoire classification with up to 47.9% AUROC improvement in few-shot learning scenarios.
title: GATTCR: A Graph Attention Network with Multi-Feature Fusion for Peripheral Blood TCR Repertoire Classification.

key_finding: Tissue-restricted secondary TCR engagement drives the transition from stem-like to CD200-expressing T helper cells, with implications for interleukin-17 production regulation.
title: Tissue-restricted secondary TCR engagement drives the transition from stem-like to CD200

key_finding: The study investigates how T-cell receptor repertoire in wild mice varies across different ages, sexes, and geographic locations in response to natural pathogen exposure.
title: The T-cell receptor repertoire of wild mice.

key_finding: Septic patients exhibit T cell exhaustion across multiple subsets, while high-risk urosepsis individuals show enhanced T cell-mediated adaptive immunity with distinct TCR repertoire profiles.
title: Distinct landscapes of T-cell immunity and TCR repertoire between sepsis and pre-septic high-risk states.

key_finding: T cell receptor repertoire characterization in aGVHD patients may provide mechanistic insights and identify predictive biomarkers for this major post-transplantation complication.
title: Quantitative evaluation of T-cell repertoire restoration following hematopoietic stem cell transplantation in patients with and without graft versus host disease.

key_finding: Strain-level antigen variations in Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron, including premature stop codons and epitope substitutions, enable immune evasion from CD4+ T cells and facilitate strain-level discrimination by the host immune system.
title: Strain-level antigen variation facilitates immune evasion in Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron.

key_finding: Shared T cell receptor α chains between individuals that recognize conserved viral peptides may differ in the number of non-germline nucleotide additions based on antigen specificity.
title: Differences in Shared T Cell receptor α Repertoire Associated with Recognition of Viral Antigens.

technology: single-cell RNA/TCR sequencing, flow cytometry, multiplex immunohistochemistry
key_finding: MR1-mediated MAIT cells play a critical role in promoting liver transplant rejection through TCR repertoire remodeling, and MR1 knockout alleviates rejection responses.
title: Single Cell Multi-Omics Revealing the Important Role of MR1 Mediated MAIT Cells in Maintaining Rejection for Liver Transplantation.

key_finding: Characterization of B cell depletion and repopulation dynamics at the single-cell level following rituximab treatment in systemic lupus erythematosus patients.
title: Single-cell level characterization of B cell depletion and repopulation following rituximab in systemic lupus erythematosus.

disease_focus: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC)
key_finding: Pre-existing TCR clones are drivers of major pathological responses in HNSCC patients treated with dual immune checkpoint inhibitors.
title: Pre-existing TCR clones drive major pathological responses in HNSCC patients treated with dual immune checkpoint inhibitors.

technology: TCR-seq (DNA-based, RNA-based, short-read, long-read, bulk, single-cell approaches); spatial TCR-seq; machine learning algorithms; motif-based clustering
key_finding: TCR-seq enables comprehensive characterization of adaptive immune repertoires and tracking of T-cell clones across temporal and spatial dimensions, providing insights into tumor-specific immune responses and treatment efficacy, though antigen specificity prediction methods require further refinement.
title: TCR sequencing in cancer immunology and immunotherapy: what, when, where, why, and how.

disease_focus: Sézary syndrome and cutaneous T-cell lymphomas
key_finding: Single-cell RNA sequencing enables dissection of malignant heterogeneity and immune dysregulation in leukaemic cutaneous T-cell lymphomas.
title: Mapping malignant T-cell states and immune circuits in Sézary syndrome by single-cell analysis.

key_finding: KD patients exhibit oligoclonal B-cell and T-cell receptor expansion with reduced diversity consistent with antigen-driven selection, whereas the CAWS mouse model shows polyclonal activation, revealing a critical divergence between human disease and current animal models.
title: Comprehensive immune repertoire profiling reveals distinct characteristics in Kawasaki disease and a mouse model.

technology: single-cell RNA sequencing, single-cell TCR sequencing
key_finding: Serum-free culture conditions support robust Vγ9Vδ2 γδ T cell expansion with higher purity, activated phenotype, and comparable or enhanced cytotoxicity compared to serum-containing conditions, offering improved consistency for adoptive cell therapy.
title: Human serum influences functional plasticity and transcriptomic landscape of γδ T cells

key_finding: TCR β CDR3 repertoire analysis in pediatric myocarditis reveals clonal expansion and disease-associated public clonotypes that provide insights into disease-associated adaptive immune responses.
title: TCR β CDR3 repertoire remodeling in pediatric myocarditis reveals clonal expansion and disease-associated public clonotypes.

technology: TCR screening, functional assay, antigen-presenting cell labeling
key_finding: A functional screening workflow successfully identified the antigen specificities of public TCRs circulating in up to 14% of individuals, revealing shared immune memories against immunodominant viral epitopes including cross-reactive coronavirus-specific and conserved influenza-specific TCRs.
title: De novo identification of the specificities of recurrently identified human T cell receptors.

key_finding: HLA risk alleles correlate with restricted TCR repertoires in T1D patients, and deep learning identified T1D-associated TCR motifs that are enriched in pancreatic lymph nodes of affected individuals.
title: Identification of a type 1 diabetes-associated T cell receptor repertoire signature from the human peripheral blood.

key_finding: AIRR-seq can detect imprints of past and ongoing SARS-CoV-2 infections through T cell receptor biomarkers, enabling monitoring of infectious disease spread based on population immunity signatures.
title: Inference of SARS-CoV-2 exposure biomarkers using large-scale T-cell repertoire profiling.

key_finding: A multi-branch ensemble learning framework enables non-invasive detection of non-small cell lung cancer through analysis of T-cell receptor sequencing from peripheral blood.
title: A multi-branch ensemble learning framework for detection of non-small cell lung cancer via T-cell receptor sequencing.

key_finding: Genetically engineered ErbB2 overexpression in organoid-derived tumors enhances sensitivity to checkpoint inhibition in a syngeneic gastric cancer model.
title: Genetically engineered ErbB2 overexpression sensitizes organoid-derived tumors to checkpoint inhibition in a syngeneic model of gastric cancer.

key_finding: T-cell receptor repertoire analysis was performed in tonsillar tissue of IgAN patients to investigate the role of T cells in the pathogenesis of this chronic glomerulonephritis.
title: Shared T-Cell Receptor Repertoire in the Tonsils of Patients with Immunoglobulin A Nephropathy.

key_finding: TCRcloud is an open-access visualization platform that enables analysis of TCR and BCR diversity changes during immune checkpoint inhibitor treatment and other cellular immune responses.
title: TCRcloud: a global visualization tool for T-cell and B-cell receptor transcripts.

key_finding: Cytokine mRNA-based therapy restores dendritic cell and T cell populations to effectively eliminate aggressive pancreatic cancer in preclinical mouse models.
title: Cytokine mRNA-based therapy alleviates dendritic cell and T cell paucity to eliminate aggressive pancreatic cancer in preclinical mouse models.

key_finding: The study examines whether herpesvirus-specific T-cell receptor sequences in patient blood associate with glioma grade and survival outcomes.
title: T-cell receptor sequencing for detection of Epstein-Barr and cytomegalovirus-specific immune responses in glioma patients: An exploratory study.

key_finding: Multi-omics profiling identifies intrathymic triggers and novel biomarkers that explain how thymoma induces myasthenia gravis through microenvironmental mechanisms.
title: Multi-omics profiling reveals intrathymic triggers and novel biomarkers in thymoma-associated myasthenia gravis.

technology: single-cell RNA-seq, T cell receptor (TCR) sequencing
disease_focus: Alzheimer's disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Parkinson's disease
key_finding: The leptomeninges contain highly clonally expanded CD8 tissue-resident memory T cells that may play a role in neuroinflammation across neurodegenerative diseases.
title: Clonal CD8

technology: transcriptomics, T cell clonality analysis, B cell clonality analysis
key_finding: Oligoclonal B cell expansion and passenger fusion genes are predictive biomarkers of nivolumab response in recurrent ovarian cancer.
title: Oligoclonal B cell expansion and passenger fusion genes predict response to Nivolumab in recurrent ovarian cancer: phase II Kyoto trial.

key_finding: TCR diversity decline with age and individual variation is governed by fundamental relationships between TCR diversity, repertoire size, and systemic clonal expansion rather than passive processes alone.
title: A fundamental relationship between TCR diversity, repertoire size and systemic clonal expansion: insights from 30,000 TCR

key_finding: T cell phenotypes in malignant pleural effusion are distinct from peripheral blood and show unique signatures associated with patient survival in mesothelioma.
title: Comparative analysis of malignant pleural effusion and peripheral blood reveals unique T cell signatures associated with survival in mesothelioma patients.

key_finding: Ten public Mtb-reactive T cell metaclones are sufficient to identify tuberculosis-specific T cell reactivity across a population, revealing population-level immunodominance of specific TCR-peptide interactions.
title: Evolution of the tuberculin skin test reveals generalisable Mtb-reactive T cell metaclones.

key_finding: High-throughput screening with Tope-seq can effectively detect cross-reactive epitopes in engineered therapeutic TCRs to assess off-target autoreactivity risk from genome-encoded self-antigens.
title: Comprehensive self-antigen screening to assess cross-reactivity in promiscuous T-cell receptors.

key_finding: High pre-treatment peripheral blood T cell receptor clonality predicts prolonged response to treatment in immune thrombocytopenia patients.
title: Immune repertoire profiling in immune thrombocytopenia: A path towards biomarkers, for The British Journal of Haematology.

key_finding: Aging reshapes the osteosarcoma tumor immune microenvironment and tumor-intrinsic programs, contributing to poor clinical outcomes in elderly patients.
title: Aging reshapes the osteosarcoma ecosystem through immune dysfunction and tumor cell reprogramming.

key_finding: Fratricide-driven, unedited CD7 CAR-T cells demonstrate preclinical efficacy in T-cell leukemia treatment through multi-omic characterization.
title: Multi-omic profiling and preclinical efficacy of fratricide-driven, unedited CD7 CAR-T cells in T-cell leukemia.

key_finding: TCR2HLA is a computational tool that accurately infers HLA genotypes from TCR repertoires by identifying HLA-associated TCR features and metaclonotypes, enabling biomarker discovery in samples lacking HLA data.
title: TCR2HLA: Calibrated inference of HLA genotypes from TCR repertoires enables identification of immunologically relevant metaclonotypes.

technology: single cell RNA-sequencing, T cell receptor (TCR)-sequencing
key_finding: T cells isolated via bronchoalveolar lavage and endobronchial brushings exhibit distinct phenotypes and clonotypes, with enrichment of CD4 T helper type 2 cells being the most significant difference between allergic asthmatics and healthy controls.
title: Distinct phenotypes and repertoires of bronchoalveolar and airway mucosal T cells in health and allergic asthma.

technology: REFLEX (single-cell multiomic profiling with paired-chain TCR sequencing, 10x FLEX platform)
key_finding: REFLEX enables massively scalable single-cell multiomic profiling of T cells with TCR sequencing, profiling 1.4M T cells from CMV-peptide-pulsed expansions and identifying novel CMV-reactive clonotypes.
title: Massively Scalable Single-cell Multiomic Profiling of T Cell Repertoire with REFLEX.

key_finding: The iHOTT model successfully recapitulates patient-specific tumor-immune interactions and demonstrates pembrolizumab-driven expansion of stem-like CD4 T cell clonotypes, supporting the need for antigen-targeted immunotherapy strategies in glioblastoma.
title: A human tumor-immune organoid model of glioblastoma.

key_finding: Convalescent COVID-19 plasma transfusion promotes inflammatory responses in CD4 memory T cells and B cells while disrupting NK cell communication with adaptive immune cells in severe COVID-19 patients.
title: Single cell transcriptomic atlas reveals distinct immune signatures following transfusion of COVID-19 convalescent plasma in severe COVID-19.

technology: HLA profiling and TCR repertoire analysis
key_finding: Investigation of HLA profiles and T-cell receptor repertoires to identify similarities and differences between idiopathic and paraneoplastic OMAS in a Spanish pediatric cohort.
title: HLA and T-Cell Receptor Investigations in Idiopathic and Paraneoplastic Opsoclonus-Myoclonus in Children.

key_finding: Recent viral infections significantly impact T cell receptor composition and influence GVHD risk independently of HLA matching in HLA-identical sibling hematopoietic cell transplantation.
title: Genetic and environmental imprints on T cell receptor repertoires as predictors of graft-versus-host disease.

key_finding: Tick-borne encephalitis virus variants induce distinct alterations in T cell receptor repertoires during acute viral infection.
title: Tick-borne encephalitis virus variants drive distinct TCR repertoire alterations.

key_finding: TCR clonality and diversity changes in peripheral blood correlate with breast cancer stage, subtype, treatment response, and survival outcomes.
title: Circulating T-cell receptor repertoire and clinicopathological correlations in breast cancer patients: immune repertoire analysis from the VGH-TAYLOR study.

key_finding: SatTCR pipeline enables saturation analysis of TCR repertoire to determine optimal sequencing depth for accurate representation of T cell repertoires in health and disease studies.
title: SatTCR: a pipeline for performing saturation analysis of the T cell receptor repertoire and a case study of a healthy canine.

key_finding: Septic patients with thrombocytopenia show upregulated T-cell activation signaling pathways and altered immune profiles associated with higher mortality and SOFA scores compared to non-thrombocytopenic septic patients.
title: Whole transcriptome analysis reveals T cell signaling activation in septic patients with thrombocytopenia.

disease_focus: multiple (immune-mediated adverse drug reactions, cancer immunotherapy, graft-versus-host disease, autoimmune disorders, food allergy, transplantation, vaccination)
key_finding: Integration of HLA polymorphisms and immune repertoire dynamics (TCR/BCR) with genetic information enables prediction of interindividual drug responses and immune-related toxicities across diverse clinical contexts.
title: Significance and challenges of immunopharmacogenomics.

technology: deep sequencing of bulk-sorted CD4+ T cells, single-cell TCR sequencing
key_finding: SjD-associated TCR motifs with shared putative antigen specificity were identified in both salivary glands and peripheral blood, with abundances correlating to reduced salivary flow and two Ro60 epitopes identified as targets of pathogenic T cell clones.
title: Identification of Sjögren's disease-associated T cell receptor motifs through deep sequencing.

disease_focus: cytomegalovirus reactivation after allogeneic haematopoietic stem cell transplantation
key_finding: Shared donor-recipient γδ T-cell phenotypic and repertoire features are associated with cytomegalovirus reactivation following allogeneic haematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
title: Shared donor-recipient γδ T-cell phenotypic and repertoire features associate with cytomegalovirus reactivation after allogeneic haematopoietic stem cell transplantation.

key_finding: Radiotherapy and immunotherapy alter the TCR repertoire by affecting hematopoietic stem cells, with combined RT and checkpoint inhibitor treatment causing notable decreases in TCRβ diversity and CDR3 diversity.
title: Analysis of the TCRβ repertoire characteristics when combining radiotherapy and immunotherapy in cancer.

key_finding: Malignant pleural effusions represent an accessible source of tumor-reactive T cells that could potentially be used for adoptive cell therapy as an alternative to surgical tumor resection.
title: Phenotypic and functional characterization of tumor-reactive T cells in malignant pleural effusions.

key_finding: Study evaluates CD8 T cell capacity to target SARS-CoV-2 variants and assess immune escape potential of HLA-A2-restricted immunodominant epitopes.
title: Resistance potential of the HLA-A2-restricted immunodominant SARS-CoV-2-specific CD8

key_finding: Pre-vaccination immune profiling identifies specific T cell, B cell, and myeloid populations that correlate with COVID-19 mRNA vaccine response in cancer patients on active treatment.
title: Pre-vaccine immune profiling in cancer patients identifies correlates of COVID-19 vaccine responses.

key_finding: The study characterizes the unique genomic organization of TCR loci in the Iberian ribbed newt, revealing separation of TRA and TRD loci, filling a critical gap in understanding TCR architecture across amphibians.
title: Unique TCR loci architecture in newts: TRA and TRD separation in

technology: REFLEX (single-cell TCR sequencing with multiome profiling integrated with 10x FLEX platform)
key_finding: REFLEX enables massively scalable single-cell profiling of >2 million T cells with paired-chain TCR sequencing and multiomic information, identifying novel CMV-reactive clonotypes and advancing understanding of T cell-mediated adaptive immunity.
title: REFLEX, a Novel Immune Profiling Assay, Combining TCR Repertoire and Multiome at Massively Scalable Single-cell Resolution to Catapult Exploration of T-cell Derived Immunity.

key_finding: In-depth characterization of CD4 TCR CDR3β repertoire diversity specific to influenza A and Epstein-Barr virus epitopes in circulating T cells.
title: Deep exploration of the TCR CDR3β repertoire specific for viral CD4 T-cell epitopes inside the circulating T-cell repertoire.

key_finding: CD4 T cells possess cytotoxic capacity in response to betaherpesviruses HHV-6B and HCMV, expanding understanding of CD4 T cell roles beyond traditional helper functions in viral control.
title: TCR repertoire of human cytotoxic CD4 T cells responding to betaherpesviruses HHV-6B and HCMV.

key_finding: Functional validation of a HER2/neu-specific TCR clonotype targeting the KIFGSLAFL peptide presented by HLA-A*02 for T cell receptor-engineered T cell therapy.
title: Anti-HER2/neu TCR-T Cells in Action: linking transcriptional signatures, secretomics, and

key_finding: HLA-DQB1*03:01 is a strong transethnic genetic determinant of earlier age of onset in type 1 narcolepsy, independent of DQA1 and ethnicity.
title: HLA-DQB1*03:01 strongly affects age of onset of type 1 narcolepsy independently of DQA1 and ethnicity.

key_finding: Cattle TCR β CDR3 repertoire shows lower diversity and higher dominant clones compared to humans and mice, with V gene rearrangement bias influenced by recombination signal sequence quality and physical distance.
title: Characterization of the Cattle TCR β CDR3 Repertoire and Gene Rearrangement Bias Influenced by Recombination Signal Sequences and Physical Distance.

technology: 10X Genomics, scRNA-seq, Smart-seq3xpress, TCR repertoire profiling
key_finding: Development of a plate-based 10X Genomics-compatible single-cell RNA-sequencing method that enables direct pairing of indexed single-cell sorting with scRNA-seq data while reducing costs for small-scale cell population profiling.
title: Plate-based 10X Genomics-compatible single-cell RNA-sequencing based on Smart-seq3xpress.

key_finding: A significantly decreased proportion of naïve MAIT cells is associated with incomplete immune reconstitution in ART-treated HIV-1 patients who are immunological non-responders.
title: A decreased proportion of naïve MAIT cells is associated with the incomplete immune reconstitution in antiretroviral therapy-treated HIV-1 patients.

key_finding: Training a generalizable TCR specificity prediction model for personalized neoepitope recognition requires 1-100 million epitopes, approximately 1000-fold larger than current publicly available datasets.
title: Assessing data size requirements for training generalizable sequence-based TCR specificity models via pan-allelic MHC-I point-mutation ligandome evaluation.

key_finding: T cell clones exhibit compartmentalized phenotypes and clonal sharing patterns between blood and tissues that vary with age and are influenced by tissue-specific antigen exposure and chronic infections.
title: Deep profiling of human T cells defines compartmentalized clones and phenotypic trajectories across blood and tonsils.

key_finding: A Transformer-based machine learning model using TCR repertoire features achieved an AUC of 0.973 for colorectal cancer diagnosis on internal testing and 0.814 on independent testing.
title: Peripheral Blood TCR Clonotype Diversity as a Biomarker for Colorectal Cancer.

key_finding: TIRTL-seq enables cost-effective paired TCR repertoire sequencing at scale by generating hundreds of TCR libraries in 384-well plates, combining the strengths of bulk and single-cell approaches while identifying clonal expansions with distinct dynamics.
title: TIRTL-seq: deep, quantitative and affordable paired TCR repertoire sequencing.

key_finding: Proliferative exhausted T cells represent a novel tumor microenvironment subset with high infiltration that correlates with improved patient survival and serves as a favorable prognostic biomarker in ESCC.
title: Discovery and validation of proliferative exhausted T cells as a favorable prognostic biomarker in esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.

key_finding: A fingerprint-based approach enables structure-based T cell receptor repertoire analysis and specificity prediction for pairing TCR with peptide-MHC complexes.
title: A fingerprint approach to pioneer structure-based T cell receptor repertoire analysis and specificity prediction.

key_finding: Natural killer T cells play an important role in shaping adaptive immune responses to influenza infection through cytokine release that affects the scope and magnitude of virus-specific T cells, B cells, and antibody responses.
title: Pigs lacking Natural Killer T cells have altered cellular responses to influenza.

key_finding: Atomistic TCR-ligand interactions regulate the differentiation of memory T cells, which provide adaptive immunity against pathogens and cancer.
title: Atomistic TCR-ligand interactions instruct memory T-cell differentiation.

key_finding: CD8+ CX3CR1+ T cells are systemically activated and infiltrate tissues in NSCLC patients treated with neoadjuvant immune checkpoint blockade.
title: Systemic activation and tissue infiltration of CD8 + CX3CR1 + T cells in non-small cell lung cancer treated with neoadjuvant immune checkpoint blockade.

key_finding: Anti-GPCR autoantibodies triggered by SARS-CoV-2 are linked to autonomic dysfunction and vasoregulation impairment in long COVID-19 patients.
title: Autonomic dysfunction and vasoregulation in long COVID-19 are linked to anti-GPCR autoantibodies.

key_finding: Lymphatic malformations exhibit immune dysfunction characterized by expansion of pro-inflammatory monocytes and atypical B cells with reduced cytotoxic T and NK cells, and S100A8 was identified as a potential therapeutic target.
title: Multidimensional single-cell analysis reveals immune dysfunction and inflammatory response in Lymphatic malformations.

key_finding: This study explores adaptive immunity dynamics in acute invasive fungal sinusitis through immune receptor repertoire analyses, addressing a previously unstudied aspect of this fatal infection in immunocompromised patients.
title: Immune Receptor Repertoire Analyses Reveal Dynamics of Adaptive Immunity in Acute Invasive Fungal Sinusitis.

key_finding: Serial TIL infusions combined with anti-PD-1 blockade achieved durable complete remission with sustained clonal persistence and reactivation of TIL-derived immunity over five years.
title: Serial TIL infusions and PD-1 blockade drive long-term clonal persistence in prostate cancer.

key_finding: Baseline tissue and circulating TCRβ repertoire composition, particularly TRBV/J gene usage, can predict pembrolizumab response and progression-free survival in advanced NSCLC patients.
title: Tissue and peripheral T cell receptor repertoire predicts immunotherapy response and progression-free survival in NSCLC patients.

key_finding: The study evaluated the safety and efficacy of allogeneic NK cell therapy combined with gemcitabine and S-1 as first-line treatment for advanced pancreatic cancer while characterizing the dynamic immune landscape associated with treatment response.
title: Dynamic transcriptional immune landscape in response to NK-cell therapy combined with gemcitabine plus S-1 in advanced pancreatic cancer: a phase 1b/2 trial.

key_finding: TCR repertoire analysis serves as a powerful biomarker for cancer diagnosis, prognosis, and therapeutic monitoring, with distinct patterns predicting immunotherapy response and clinical outcomes.
title: Harnessing TCR repertoires: predictive insights and therapeutic monitoring in cancer immunotherapy.

technology: bulk TCR sequencing, single-cell RNA sequencing
key_finding: PDI-TCR assay successfully identifies and distinguishes antigen-specific TCRs from bystander TCRs in TB patients, revealing that Mtb-specific T cells display diverse short-lived effector phenotypes at diagnosis that transition to memory phenotypes during treatment.
title: Peptide-driven identification of TCRs reveals dynamics and phenotypes of CD4 T cells in tuberculosis.

key_finding: Oncolytic measles virus encoding neutrophil-activating protein demonstrates significant anti-tumor effects in synovial sarcoma models, addressing the immunologically cold nature of this malignancy.
title: Oncolytic measles virotherapy encoding the neutrophil-activating protein is effective in synovial sarcoma.

technology: bulk TCR-seq (SMARTer Human TCR Profiling Kit with UMIs)
key_finding: TCR repertoire analysis reveals distinct signatures in reinfected individuals compared to primary infection and healthy controls, with both groups showing significantly reduced TCR diversity.
title: Distinct characteristics of T cell receptor repertoire associated with the SARS-CoV-2 reinfection.

key_finding: CD4+ MAIT cells express highly diverse TRAV1-2- TCRs including TRAV21, TRAV8, and TRAV12 families, distinct from the canonical TRAV1-2+ CD8+ MAIT cell subset.
title: CD4+ mucosal-associated invariant T cells express highly diverse T cell receptors.

key_finding: A method for analyzing TCR and IgG immune repertoires from bulk RNA-seq data of PBMCs, focusing on CDR3 sequence diversity to understand host adaptive immune responses to T. cruzi infection in Rhesus macaques.
title: Profiling T Cell Receptor and Immunoglobulin Repertoires Through RNA Sequencing to Understand Host Response to Trypanosoma cruzi Infection.

technology: TCR repertoire sequencing, bulk transcriptomics, flow cytometry
key_finding: Psoriasis vulgaris drives dynamic remodeling of γδ T cell repertoires in both blood and skin that is shaped by disease severity, age, and sex, with circulating γδ T cells adopting an activated, cytotoxic, tissue-homing phenotype.
title: Psoriasis vulgaris leaves a dynamic imprint on circulating and skin γδ TCR repertoires shaped by disease severity, age, and sex.

key_finding: DeepTAPE, a deep learning framework analyzing TCRβ CDR3 sequences, achieves robust classification performance for SLE diagnosis and enables discovery of immunological biomarkers.
title: Deep learning-driven TCRβ repertoire analysis enhances diagnosis and enables mining of immunological biomarkers in systemic lupus erythematosus.

key_finding: TCR repertoire analysis integrated with clinical and imaging data (TCRnodseek plus) demonstrates superior diagnostic performance for distinguishing benign from malignant indeterminate pulmonary nodules.
title: Large-Scale T-cell Receptor Repertoire Profiling Unveils Tumor-Specific Signals for Diagnosing Indeterminate Pulmonary Nodules.

key_finding: HTGTS-TCR-seq enables efficient quantitative profiling of TCR α and β gene rearrangements, revealing stage-specific V and J usage, age-associated repertoire alterations, and conserved CDR3 features across mouse and human T cells.
title: HTGTS-TCR-Seq for Profiling of Mouse and Human T-Cell Receptor α and β Gene Rearrangements and Diversity.

technology: next-generation sequencing of TCR β chain (TCRB)
disease_focus: multiple myeloma, monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance, smoldering multiple myeloma
key_finding: Machine learning analysis revealed distinct TCR clusters with different amino acid properties that differentiate healthy individuals from patients with plasma cell dyscrasias, despite no significant differences in overall TCR repertoire diversity.
title: Machine learning reveals distinct T-cell receptor clusters in plasma cell dyscrasias compared to healthy controls.

key_finding: Autoreactive T cells in anti-Jo1+ antisynthetase syndrome patients recognize a novel epitope on histidyl t-RNA synthetase.
title: Autoreactive T cells identified in patients with anti-Jo1+ antisynthetase syndrome recognise a new epitope on histidyl t-RNA synthetase.

technology: single-cell RNA sequencing, single-cell TCR sequencing
disease_focus: thymic involution, aging, cancer and infection vulnerability
key_finding: Aging reduces T-lineage potential in thymic progenitors while increasing innate lymphoid potential, leading to peripheral T cell aging characterized by reduced naive T cells and shifts in TCR repertoire diversity.
title: Single-cell analysis of human thymus and peripheral blood unveils the dynamics of T cell development and aging.

key_finding: TCR clonotype patterns vary across disease stages and histologic subtypes in cutaneous T-cell lymphomas, with potential applications in risk stratification.
title: T-Cell Receptor Clonotypes and Aggressive Subtypes in Cutaneous T-Cell Lymphoma.

disease_focus: chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP) as adverse effect of cilta-cel therapy
key_finding: CIDP is a severe complication of cilta-cel therapy that may arise from bystander expansion of autoreactive CD8⁺ T-cells rather than direct CAR-T cell activity, and early T-cell-depleting therapy may improve outcomes.
title: Chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP) after cilta-cel therapy.

key_finding: NLP-based clustering of CDR3 sequences using Word2Vec and KMeans successfully distinguished immune activation states in ARDS patients from controls based on repertoire structural topology, with ARDS samples showing high dispersion and disrupted organization compared to tight clusters in healthy controls.
title: Optimizing clustering of CDR3 sequences using natural language processing, Word2Vec, and KMeans.

key_finding: A TCR meta-database was created to identify SARS-CoV-2 epitope-specific T cells and determine whether mRNA vaccination influences the T cell repertoire diversity and complexity.
title: Patterns of restricted TCR usage following SARS-CoV-2 vaccination and severe disease.

key_finding: Downregulated tumor lysine metabolism reshapes the immune microenvironment and influences therapeutic responses in hepatocellular carcinoma.
title: Multi-omics profiling reveals downregulated tumor lysine metabolism reshaping the immune microenvironment and therapeutic responses in hepatocellular carcinoma.

key_finding: Scirpy is a Python package that enables integrated analysis of multi-modal single-cell RNA and TCR sequencing data for comprehensive characterization of T-cell repertoires, antigen specificity, and clonal dynamics.
title: Analysis of single-cell TCR repertoires and gene expression from multi-modal scRNA-seq data.
Infiltration characteristics and regulatory mechanisms of CD8

key_finding: Chemotherapy acts as a broad antigen-releasing agent that synergizes with TLR7/8/9 agonists to generate potent antitumor immunity in immunologically cold tumors.
title: Chemotherapy Liberates a Broadening Repertoire of Tumor Antigens for TLR7/8/9-Mediated Potent Antitumor Immunity.

disease_focus: combined immunodeficiency (CID) and severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID)
key_finding: A reliable clinical TCRβ sequencing strategy was developed with established reference values across healthy populations that can differentiate pathological T cell repertoire characteristics in patients with RAG-SCID/CID.
title: A High-Throughput Sequencing Strategy for Clinical Repertoire Profiling of T Cell Receptor Beta Chain: Development and Reference Values Across Healthy Adults, Paediatrics, and Cord Blood Units.

technology: single-cell TCR sequencing, single-cell mRNA sequencing
disease_focus: yellow fever vaccination; HIV-1 infection; B cell malignancy
key_finding: Yellow fever vaccine-induced CD8+ T cells with TCR bias can be successfully redirected toward unrelated target cells including HIV-1-infected cells through bispecific T cell engagers.
title: TCR bias drives development of dominant vaccine-induced CD8+ T cell responses which can be redirected toward cellular targets.

key_finding: Umbilical cord blood-derived cytotoxic T lymphocytes can target melanoma through HLA-A2-restricted tumor antigens, offering an alternative cell source to peripheral blood for adoptive cell therapy.
title: Umbilical cord blood-derived cytotoxic T lymphocytes target melanoma via HLA-A2-restricted tumour antigens.

technology: scRNA-seq, single-cell TCR-seq, paired TCRαβ sequencing
key_finding: A large-scale reference of paired single-cell RNA/TCR sequencing from over 2 million T cells reveals intrinsic features of germline-encoded TCR-MHC restriction and links TCRαβ information to T cell phenotypes across multiple disease conditions at the population level.
title: A pan-disease and population-level single-cell TCRαβ repertoire reference.

key_finding: AIMS is a bioinformatics tool that enables unbiased analysis of immunopeptidomic datasets by identifying biophysical signatures and elucidating differences in MHC peptide repertoires across tissues and conditions.
title: Utilizing Protein Bioinformatics to Delve Deeper into Immunopeptidomic Datasets with AIMS: An Automated Immune Molecule Separator.

key_finding: AutoTFCNNY, a multi-instance neural network combining Transformer and CNN, achieves average AUC >0.94 for cancer detection across 22 cancer types using peripheral blood TCR repertoires.
title: AutoTFCNNY: A multi-instance neural network for enhanced early cancer detection using TCR data.

key_finding: Peripheral TCR repertoire alterations in epilepsy reflect adaptive immune activation and may serve as immune signatures for disease diagnosis and brain atrophy severity.
title: Systemic T Cell Receptor Profiling Reveals Adaptive Immune Activation and Potential Immune Signatures of Diagnosis and Brain Atrophy in Epilepsy.

title: Comprehensive analysis of αβT-cell receptor repertoires reveals signatures of thymic selection.

technology: single-cell RNA sequencing, single-cell protein sequencing, single-cell TCR sequencing, ELISpot, flow cytometry
key_finding: Vaccination-induced CD8 T cell repertoires maintain polyclonality with high-avidity TCRs, which provides robustness against viral escape through epitope alterations rather than relying on clonal expansion differences.
title: Vaccination-induced T cell responses maintain polyclonality with high antigen receptor avidity.

key_finding: The study investigates the long-term persistence and evolution of memory T-cell responses to SARS-CoV-2 spike antigen following primary infection and subsequent antigen exposures.
title: Long-persisting SARS-CoV-2 spike-specific CD4

key_finding: Incomplete abstract provided - unable to extract key findings
title: Neuron-reactive KIR

title: Single-Cell TCR Sequencing Uncovers Remodeling of the Immune Repertoire After a Short-Term Gluten-Free Diet in Pediatric Celiac Disease.

key_finding: ALICE and TCRNET algorithms outperform GLIPH2 and tcrdist3 for identifying antigen-specific T-cell receptors using neighborhood enrichment approaches.
title: Neighborhood enrichment for the identification of antigen-specific T-cell receptors.

technology: TCR immune repertoire sequencing, immunohistochemical analysis

key_finding: Tertiary lymphoid structures in Merkel cell carcinoma promote infiltration of naïve and central memory T cells, which correlates with improved immunotherapy response to immune checkpoint inhibition.
title: Tertiary lymphoid structures in Merkel cell carcinoma facilitate naïve and central memory T-cell infiltration linked to immunotherapy response.

key_finding: Blood memory CD8 T cell phenotypes can predict immune checkpoint inhibitor treatment responses in lung cancer patients.
title: Blood memory CD8 T cell phenotypes in lung cancer patients predict immune checkpoint treatment responses.

key_finding: ESPEC-SUIT is a versatile assay that enables identification and tracking of polyclonal, antigen-specific T cell receptors from both MHC class I and class II-restricted responses in cancer patients.
title: ESPEC-SUIT: a versatile and robust platform to identify and track antigen-specific T cell receptors in patients with cancer.

key_finding: Dual checkpoint blockade with anti-PD-1 and anti-LAG-3 promotes expansion of tumor-reactive T cell clones along a unique differentiation pathway in glioblastoma patients.
title: Dual checkpoint blockade of glioblastoma with Anti-PD-1 and Anti-LAG-3 promotes expansion of tumor-reactive T cell clones along a unique pathway of differentiation.

key_finding: THLANet, a deep learning model using ESM-2 embeddings, accurately predicts TCR-neoantigen binding specificity for class I HLA-presented antigens, enabling assessment of immunogenic potential in cancer immunotherapy.
title: THLANet: A deep learning framework for predicting TCR-pHLA binding in immunotherapy applications.

key_finding: Population-level analysis of TCR-HLA interactions provides foundational mapping of how T-cell receptors interact with peptides presented by human leukocyte antigens.
title: Large-scale statistical mapping of T-cell receptor

key_finding: TCRdb2.0 is an expanded T-cell receptor sequence database containing approximately 700 million TCR sequences from nearly 20,000 samples across 46 tissues and 147 clinical conditions, with enhanced functionality for browsing and analysis.
title: TCRdb 2.0: an updated T-cell receptor sequence database.

key_finding: DDX55 suppresses activation-promoting transposable elements to maintain naïve T cell homeostasis and preserve TCR repertoire diversity.
title: DDX55 safeguards naïve T cell homeostasis by suppressing activation-promoting transposable elements.

title: Tissue-specific clonal selection and differentiation of CD4

key_finding: TET2 and TET3 deletion impacts TCRα and TCRβ repertoire diversity in murine CD4 T cells in both thymic and peripheral compartments.
title: Assessing the impact of TET2 and TET3 deletion in TCRalpha and TCRbeta repertoire in murine CD4 T cells in physiological and pathophysiological conditions.

key_finding: HLA-A*02:01 presents penicillin-modified cysteinylated peptides that can be recognized by drug-specific T cells, providing mechanistic insights into immune-mediated β-lactam hypersensitivity reactions.
title: HLA-A*02:01 Presents Penicillin-Modified Cysteinylated Peptides for T Cell Recognition.

key_finding: Tumor-derived T cell clonotypes are enriched in cell-free DNA and persist in peripheral blood during pembrolizumab treatment, suggesting cfDNA TCR repertoire analysis can serve as a non-invasive biomarker for tumor-infiltrating immune responses.
title: T cell receptor repertoire in cell-free DNA as a proxy for tumor infiltrates in patients treated with pembrolizumab.

key_finding: Spatial distribution of T-cell clones in NSCLC tumors provides insights into immunogenic mechanisms and may have prognostic and predictive implications for immunotherapy response.
title: Spatial TCR clonality and clonal expansion in the in situ microenvironment of non-small cell lung cancer.

key_finding: Tseek enables characterization of canine TCR repertoires with minimal bias to identify influences of genetic lineage and age and monitor immune status in dogs.
title: The canine T cell receptor repertoire.

key_finding: Traditional neo-antigen-focused methods for detecting tumor-reactive CD8+ TILs in pancreatic cancer are limited in scope and complexity, necessitating alternative approaches to better understand the biological characteristics of these cells.
title: Functional tumor-reactive CD8 + T cells in pancreatic cancer.

key_finding: Protein language models applied to TCR repertoires capture context-dependent features from unlabeled data and achieve superior generalization performance for antigen specificity prediction compared to conventional sequence representation methods.
title: TCR representation learning with protein language models: a comprehensive review.

key_finding: Zanubrutinib combined with rituximab and lenalidomide achieved a 65% complete response rate in elderly DLBCL patients, with efficacy primarily driven by tumor microenvironmental alterations and T-cell immunological memory rather than genetic changes.
title: A phase 2 study of zanubrutinib in combination with rituximab and lenalidomide in de novo diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.

key_finding: TCR alpha and beta chain V-region loops contain amino acid signatures that distinguish MHC class I from class II recognition, with machine learning models achieving improved accuracy when both chains are analyzed.
title: T-cell receptor insights: Determinants of Major Histocompatibility Complex class I versus class II recognition.

key_finding: TCR-focused approaches using patient TCR repertoire analysis can complement antigen-focused strategies to select therapeutic TCRs for personalized adoptive cell therapy in cancer immunotherapy.
title: Exploiting TCR Repertoire Analysis to Select Therapeutic TCRs for Cancer Immunotherapy.

technology: TCR screening, viral genome screening, antigen-presenting cell labeling
key_finding: A functional screening workflow identified target specificities of public TCRs circulating in up to 14% of individuals, including cross-reactive seasonal coronavirus TCRs and conserved epitope-targeting TCRs for influenza and herpesvirus infections.

disease_focus: T-cell lymphoma, diffuse large B-cell lymphoma
key_finding: A clonally expanded T-cell population with TFH-like characteristics developed 2.5 years post-CAR-T therapy but spontaneously resolved, highlighting the need to distinguish post-CAR-T clonal lymphoproliferations from true malignant transformation.
title: A clonally expanded nodal T-cell population diagnosed as T-cell lymphoma after CAR-T therapy.

key_finding: Disease severity in COVID-19 is associated with progressive CD8+ T cell and Treg depletion, increased CD4+ T cells and plasma cells, dysregulated immune pathways, and reduced TCR/BCR repertoire diversity.
title: Severity-specific immune landscape of COVID-19 revealed by single-cell sequencing.

key_finding: γδ T cells were characterized in peripheral blood from melanoma patients using ultrasensitive DNA sequencing and flow cytometry to inform development of γδ T cell-based immunotherapies.
title: Cellular and molecular characterization of γδ T cells in peripheral blood from patients with metastases from cutaneous and uveal melanoma.

technology: ['spleen transcriptome sequencing', 'TCR β CDR3 repertoire sequencing']
key_finding: IGFBP7 plays a role in immune regulation and modulation of PCOS-like symptoms through effects on immune gene expression and T cell receptor diversity in a DHEA-induced mouse model.
title: The role of IGFBP7 in the immune regulation of PCOS-like symptoms based on spleen transcriptome and TCR β CDR3 repertoire analysis.

title: TCR2HLA: calibrated inference of HLA genotypes from TCR repertoires enables identification of immunologically relevant metaclonotypes.

key_finding: Genetic variants in RC3H1 that disrupt Roquin-1 repressor activity cause primary immunodeficiency characterized by susceptibility to infections, hyperinflammation, autoimmunity, and lymphoproliferation through dysregulation of posttranscriptional mRNA stability.
title: Autosomal-dominant Roquin-1 immunodeficiency and hyperinflammation.

title: Genomic specificity of anti-mouse TCR mAbs determined by single-cell RNAseq.

key_finding: The insulator EACBE regulates V(D)J recombination of the Tcrd gene by modulating chromatin organization to generate T cell receptor diversity.
title: The insulator EACBE regulates V(D)J recombination of Tcrd gene by modulating chromatin organization.

key_finding: A comprehensive dataset of pre-treatment TCR repertoires from 215 newly diagnosed colorectal cancer patients reveals immune system characteristics useful for immunology research, biomarker discovery, and risk assessment.
title: A Large-Scale Dataset of Pre-Treatment TCR Repertoires in Colorectal Cancer Patients.

key_finding: Characterization of tumor-specific CD8 T cell responses in hormone receptor-positive breast cancers to understand immune mechanisms in ER+ tumors.
title: Tumor-specific CD8 T cell characterization in HR

key_finding: Increased TCR degeneracy in HTLV-1-specific CTLs, particularly at position 5 of the antigen, is inversely correlated with HTLV-1 proviral load and enhances antiviral efficacy against variant viruses.
title: High TCR Degeneracy Enhances Antiviral Efficacy of HTLV-1-Specific CTLs by Targeting Variant Viruses in HAM Patients.

technology: 10x Genomics VDJ sequencing, single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNAseq), TCR and BCR enrichment

key_finding: Temporal dynamics and diversity of the T cell receptor repertoire are associated with recovery in patients with severe COVID-19.
title: Temporal TCR dynamics and epitope diversity mark recovery in severe COVID-19 patients.

key_finding: Integration of single-cell transcriptomic and chromatin accessibility landscapes reveals heterogeneous cellular properties and gene regulatory programs in t(8;21) AML.
title: A multi-omic single-cell landscape reveals transcription and epigenetic regulatory features of t(8;21) AML.

key_finding: Integration of single-cell RNA-sequencing and adaptive immune receptor sequencing is a powerful approach for studying lymphocyte development.

Circulating T-cell receptor repertoire functional unit analysis detected nearly 50% of stage I lung cancers at 80% specificity and improved sensitivity by up to 20 percentage points when combined with ctDNA and protein biomarkers.
title: Circulating T-cell receptor repertoire for cancer early detection.

key_finding: The iHOTT model enables study of patient-specific tumor-immune interactions in glioblastoma and demonstrates that pembrolizumab treatment induces stem-like CD4-T-cell clonotype expansion, supporting the need for antigen-targeted immunotherapy strategies.

key_finding: Combined cytokine profiling and T-cell receptor repertoire analysis reveals correlations between T-cell repertoire composition and cytokine imbalance in MS patients, providing insights into MS-driving immune responses.

title: Napsin A-specific T-cell clonotypes are associated with improved clinical outcomes in patients receiving checkpoint immunotherapy for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer.

key_finding: Peripheral blood TCR repertoire composition prior to treatment may serve as a predictive biomarker for clinical response in NSCLC patients receiving immunochemotherapy.

technology: bulk RNA-seq, whole exome sequencing, TCR repertoire analysis

title: The differential immunological impact of photon vs proton radiation therapy in high-grade lymphopenia in patients with gastrointestinal tumors.

technology: micro-well-based single-cell multi-omics platform, scRNA-seq, targeted cell surface proteomics, AIRR sequencing, dextramers

technology: whole exome sequencing, whole genome sequencing

title: Association of antibody and T cell receptor repertoires in Trypanosoma cruzi infected rhesus macaques and host response to infection.

key_finding: Integrative genomic and transcriptomic analysis identifies targetable vulnerabilities in angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma.
title: Integrative Genomic and Transcriptomic Analysis Reveals Targetable Vulnerabilities in Angioimmunoblastic T-Cell Lymphoma.

key_finding: Integration of T cell repertoire, mass cytometry, and genetic data using Latent Class Model analysis reveals immunological subphenotypes in COVID-19 patients with varying clinical outcomes.

key_finding: HLA-DQ monomers successfully enriched HLA-DQ-specific CD4 T cells through repeated stimulation, as demonstrated by proliferation and activation marker upregulation.

key_finding: A joint experimental and computational approach was developed to accurately predict antigen specificity of clinically-derived TCR sequences using biophysical modeling.
title: Biophysical modeling for accurate T cell specificity prediction of viral and tumor antigens.

key_finding: Phage display combined with machine learning enables discovery of cancer antigen-specific TCRs targeting epitopes like NY-ESO-1 for T cell-based therapies.
title: Phage display enables machine learning discovery of cancer antigen-specific TCRs.

technology: whole exome sequencing, intracellular cytokine staining, TCR repertoire sequencing, single-cell transcriptomics
key_finding: Personalized neoantigen-targeting peptide vaccines induced robust, polyfunctional, and polyclonal CD4+ and CD8+ T cell responses against mutIDH1 in 89% of patients, with shared TCR sequences identified across patients with matching HLA alleles.
title: In-depth characterization of vaccine-induced neoantigen-specific T cells in patients with IDH1-mutant glioma undergoing personalized peptide vaccination.

key_finding: Different sampling methods (BAL, endobronchial brushings, biopsies) capture distinct T cell phenotypes and clonotypes from the lungs in health and allergic asthma.
title: Distinct phenotypes and repertoires of bronchoalveolar and airway mucosal T cells in health and allergic asthma.

key_finding: Enhanced mutanome analysis can improve neoepitope identification and T-cell response induction for personalized immunotherapy of pancreatic cancer, addressing challenges of low tumor cell content and limited mutational load.
title: Enhanced mutanome analysis towards the induction of neoepitope-reactive T-cell responses for personalized immunotherapy of pancreatic cancer.

key_finding: LightCTL, a lightweight contrastive learning method with context-aware prompts, substantially improves the accuracy and generalizability of TCR-pMHC binding specificity prediction across diverse datasets and TCR sequence variations.
title: LightCTL: lightweight contrastive TCR-pMHC specificity learning with context-aware prompt.

key_finding: BCR and TCR repertoires identified as potential prognostic biomarkers for pediatric B-ALL patients to improve individualized therapy and clinical outcomes.
title: High-throughput Sequencing Reveals that BCR and TCR Repertoires as Potential Prognostic Biomarkers for Pediatric Patients with B-ALL.

key_finding: A two-stage VAE framework with contrastive learning and cosine triplet loss improved TCR latent feature representation and peptide-specific clustering, outperforming sequence-based methods for TCR specificity prediction.
title: TCRCluster: a novel approach to T-cell receptor latent featurization and clustering using contrastive learning-guided two-stage variational autoencoders.

key_finding: Generative Adversarial Networks using LSTM and LeakyReLU architectures can generate biologically reliable and diverse CDR3 sequences for engineered T-cell receptor applications.
title: High-fidelity in silico generation and augmentation of TCR repertoire data using generative adversarial networks.

technology: massive parallel sequencing, single-cell experiment platforms, genome engineering
key_finding: Recent advancements in big data science and sequencing technologies enable comprehensive analysis of TCR repertoire diversity and enable TCR-mediated immune regulation research including TCR as biomarkers, TCR-T cell differentiation links, and TCR genetic regulation.
title: Big Data Science on T Cell Receptor-mediated Immune Regulation.

key_finding: Spatiotemporal T-cell tracking enables personalized TCR T-cell therapy design to improve immune checkpoint inhibition responses in pediatric malignancies with low mutational burden.
title: Spatiotemporal T-cell tracking for personalized T-cell receptor T-cell therapy designs in childhood cancer.

technology: multiplex PCR, TCR-seq, unique molecular identifiers
key_finding: A robust multiplex PCR protocol (FUME-TCRseq) with unique molecular identifiers enables TCR repertoire sequencing from poor-quality RNA in formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded tumor samples.
title: A Robust Quantitative Multiplex PCR for T-cell Receptor Repertoire Sequencing from Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded Samples.

key_finding: Immunopipe is a comprehensive pipeline for integrated analysis of scRNA-seq and scTCR-seq data that combines command-line and web-based interfaces to enable exploration of cell heterogeneity and T-cell receptor repertoires.
title: Immunopipe: a comprehensive and flexible scRNA-seq and scTCR-seq data analysis pipeline.

technology: peptide-MHC multimers, TCR gene transfer, combinatorial peptide library scanning
key_finding: KLK4-A2 TCR demonstrates high specificity and effective cytotoxic killing potential against prostate cancer with minimal off-target reactivity, supporting its therapeutic potential for TCR-based immunotherapy.
title: TCR-Based Therapy Directed against Kallikrein-Related Peptidase 4 Is Safe and Effective against Prostate Cancer.

disease_focus: colorectal cancer and non-small-cell lung cancer
key_finding: T-cell receptor diversity (D50) values in peripheral blood may serve as a biomarker for diagnosis and treatment evaluation in colorectal and lung cancers.
title: Peripheral Blood T-Cell Receptor Repertoire Diversity as a Potential Biomarker in the Diagnosis and Treatment Evaluation of Colorectal and Lung Cancers: A Prospective Observational Study.
